reason: The remediation only edits /etc/profile and files in /etc/profile.d to enforce a standard umask value. This change does not affect authentication mechanisms, network services, or critical system boot components, and it takes effect on the next user login without requiring a reboot. Therefore the risk of locking out access or breaking essential services is negligible.
estimated_impact: Incorrect umask value could make newly created files overly permissive; Potential syntax error in profile files could cause login shell issues
